description:
You are a performance engineer who has been asked to reclaim some disk-space taken up by large, stale log files produced by a set of test applications.

    Current situation
    -----------------
    1. Log root directory:  /home/user/apps/logs
       ├── app1.log              (exactly 614 400 bytes, mtime ≥ 3 days ago)
       ├── app2.log              (exactly 307 200 bytes, mtime ≥ 3 days ago)
       └── old/
           └── old.log           (exactly 819 200 bytes, mtime ≥ 5 days ago)

    2. Reporting directory:     /home/user/output
       (This directory already exists and is writable by you.)

    Requested work
    --------------
    a. Using any safe combination of 2–3 commands that rely on the standard
       GNU tools “find” and “xargs”, locate every “*.log” file inside the
       tree /home/user/apps/logs that meets **both** of the following
       criteria:
         • size strictly greater than 512 kB (524 288 bytes)  
         • last modification time strictly older than 2 days  
       (The two files »app1.log« and »old.log« satisfy these criteria,
       whereas »app2.log« does not.)

    b. Compress every file selected in step (a) with gzip and remove the
       uncompressed version in the same operation (i.e. each matching file
       should end up as “filename.log.gz” in exactly the same directory
       where the “*.log” file originally lived).

    c. Produce a plain-text report file called  
       /home/user/output/compression_report.txt

       • One line per file that was actually compressed.  
       • Each line must contain exactly three TAB-separated fields:  
            1. absolute path of the original “*.log” file  
            2. size in bytes before compression  
            3. size in bytes after compression (the size of the “*.gz”)  
       • Lines must appear in lexicographic order of the first field.  
       • The file must terminate with a single trailing newline (Unix “\n”).  

       Example of the required layout (numbers shown here are ONLY an
       illustration ⟨do not hard-code them⟩):

           /home/user/apps/logs/app1.log<TAB>614400<TAB>12345\n
           /home/user/apps/logs/old/old.log<TAB>819200<TAB>23456\n

       Make sure to respect the exact positioning of TABs and the final
       newline because the automated grader will check this byte-for-byte.

    Constraints & hints
    -------------------
    • Use “find” only once and keep the total number of commands to 2 – 3.  
    • Do not use sudo; everything must run as the normal “user”.  
    • The gzip utility is already available in the container.  
    • After successful execution, »app1.log« and »old.log« must no longer
      exist as uncompressed files, while their “.gz” replacements must be
      present and readable.  
    • The small file »app2.log« must be left untouched (still uncompressed).  

    Test validation rules (informative)
    -----------------------------------
    The grader will verify that:
      1. /home/user/apps/logs/app1.log.gz and
         /home/user/apps/logs/old/old.log.gz both exist,
         and their uncompressed counterparts are gone.
      2. /home/user/apps/logs/app2.log remains uncompressed.
      3. /home/user/output/compression_report.txt exists, has exactly two
         lines, matches the required TAB-separated layout, the first two
         numeric fields are 614400 and 819200 respectively, and the third
         numeric field of every line is **strictly smaller** than its
         second field.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-populated artefacts in the container BEFORE the agent starts
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <!-- directory layout -->
        /home/user/apps/logs/
        /home/user/apps/logs/old/
        /home/user/output/

        <!-- files with deterministic size and content -->
        /home/user/apps/logs/app1.log
            size_bytes="614400"
            contents="repetition of the single byte 0x41 (ASCII 'A') 614 400 times"
            mtime="3 days ago"

        /home/user/apps/logs/app2.log
            size_bytes="307200"
            contents="repetition of the single byte 0x42 (ASCII 'B') 307 200 times"
            mtime="3 days ago"

        /home/user/apps/logs/old/old.log
            size_bytes="819200"
            contents="repetition of the single byte 0x43 (ASCII 'C') 819 200 times"
            mtime="5 days ago"
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        EXPECTED STATE AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
        (numbers for “after_bytes” will be measured by the test at runtime;
         they only need to be strictly smaller than “before_bytes”)
    -->
    <postconditions>
        <!-- compressed files must exist -->
        /home/user/apps/logs/app1.log.gz        must_exist="yes"
        /home/user/apps/logs/old/old.log.gz     must_exist="yes"

        <!-- original large logs must be gone -->
        /home/user/apps/logs/app1.log           must_exist="no"
        /home/user/apps/logs/old/old.log        must_exist="no"

        <!-- small log must stay untouched -->
        /home/user/apps/logs/app2.log           must_exist="yes"
        /home/user/apps/logs/app2.log.gz        must_exist="no"

        <!-- report file -->
        /home/user/output/compression_report.txt
            must_exist="yes"
            must_have_two_lines="yes"
            line1_prefix="/home/user/apps/logs/app1.log\t614400\t"
            line2_prefix="/home/user/apps/logs/old/old.log\t819200\t"
            numeric_third_field_smaller_than_second="yes"
    </postconditions>
name: task_000000_9ba2b0a3
